feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! 'Ti va?' è il modo più comune per invitare qualcuno.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'ti va?' significa 'do you feel like it?'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! 'Ti va?' is the most common way to invite someone.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'ti va?' means 'do you feel like it?'.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'beccarsi' in questo contesto significa incontrarsi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! 'Beccarsi' is very colloquial.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'beccarsi' in this context means to meet up.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Significa passare velocemente in un posto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'fare un salto' significa passare a trovare qualcuno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! It means to stop by a place quickly.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'fare un salto' means to drop by and see someone.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! 'Sei libero?' è fondamentale per iniziare un invito.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'libero' significa 'free'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! 'Are you free?' is essential for starting an invitation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'libero' means 'free'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! È il contrario di libero.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'impegnato' significa che hai già qualcosa da fare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It's the opposite of free.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'impegnato' means you already have something to do.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! 'Magari!' si usa spesso per accettare con entusiasmo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'magari' esprime desiderio o possibilità.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! 'Magari!' is often used to accept enthusiastically.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'magari' expresses desire or possibility.

feedback.correctItalian: Perfetto! Il classico rito sociale italiano.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'aperitivo si fa prima di cena.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! The classic Italian social ritual.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the aperitif is done before dinner.

feedback.correctItalian: Sì! Molto colloquiale per dire 'qualcosa da bere'.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si riferisce all'atto di bere socialmente.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! Very colloquial for 'something to drink'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it refers to the act of social drinking.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Si usa per chiedere conferma di partecipazione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'ci stai?' chiede se la persona vuole partecipare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It's used to ask for confirmation of participation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'ci stai?' asks if the person wants to participate.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! 'Dare pacco' significa non presentarsi a un appuntamento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, in gergo sociale un 'pacco' è un bidone.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! 'Dare pacco' means not showing up to an appointment.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, in social slang a 'pacco' is a flake-out.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! 'Fare serata' significa uscire la sera.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la 'serata' è l'intero periodo passato fuori.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! 'Fare serata' means going out for the evening.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'serata' is the whole period spent out.

feedback.correctItalian: Sì! 'Facciamo un giro?' è un classico invito informale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, un 'giro' è una passeggiata o un'uscita breve.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! 'Facciamo un giro?' is a classic informal invitation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, a 'giro' is a walk or a short outing.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! 'Conosco un bel posto' si dice sempre.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'posto' è un termine generico per un locale o luogo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! 'I know a nice place' is said all the time.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'posto' is a generic term for a venue or location.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Si usa per descrivere un locale o un'idea.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'carino' significa piacevole.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! It's used to describe a venue or an idea.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'carino' means pleasant.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! È il modo standard per accettare un invito.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'volentieri' indica che accetti con piacere.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It's the standard way to accept an invitation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'volentieri' indicates you accept with pleasure.

feedback.correctItalian: Sì! Si usa per iniziare un rifiuto gentile.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'purtroppo' esprime dispiacere.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! It's used to start a polite refusal.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'purtroppo' expresses regret.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! 'Facciamo il solito posto?' 'Sì, lo stesso'.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'stesso' significa identico.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! 'Let's do the usual place?' 'Yes, the same one'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'stesso' means identical.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! 'Il solito bar' è dove si va sempre.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'solito' è quello che si fa abitualmente.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! 'The usual bar' is where one always goes.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'solito' is what is habitually done.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Può essere formale o informale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, un 'appuntamento' è un incontro fissato.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It can be formal or informal.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, an 'appuntamento' is a scheduled meeting.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'indirizzo' è dove si trova un posto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! 'I'll send you the address' is fundamental.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'address' is where a place is located.

feedback.correctItalian: Sì! Ormai si manda sempre 'la posizione' su WhatsApp.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si riferisce alla posizione geografica digitale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! Nowadays 'the location' is always sent on WhatsApp.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it refers to the digital geographic location.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'messaggio' è testo scritto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! 'Write me a message'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'message' is written text.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, un 'vocale' è un messaggio audio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Italians love sending 'vocali'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, a 'vocale' is an audio message.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è il tempo tra pranzo e cena.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! 'Shall we meet in the afternoon?'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the time between lunch and dinner.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Il momento tipico per uscire.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'sera' è dopo il tramonto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! The typical time to go out.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'evening' is after sunset.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è il quinto giorno della settimana.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! The start of the weekend.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the fifth day of the week.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è il giorno prima di domenica.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The day of parties.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the day before Sunday.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Giorno di relax o gite fuori porta.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è l'ultimo giorno della settimana.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Day of relaxation or trips out of town.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the last day of the week.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'settimana' sono 7 giorni.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! 'What are you doing this week?'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'week' is 7 days.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'prossimo' indica quello che viene dopo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! 'Next Saturday'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'next' indicates what comes after.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'scorso' indica quello passato.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! 'Last weekend'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'scorso' indicates the past one.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la cena si mangia la sera.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! 'I invite you to dinner'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, dinner is eaten in the evening.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è il piatto tondo più famoso.
feedback.correctEnglish: Easy! The most classic invitation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the most famous round dish.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è una bevanda alcolica comune.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! 'Shall we have a beer?'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a common alcoholic drink.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'espresso italiano.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! The fastest social invitation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the Italian espresso.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dove si guardano i film.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! 'Shall we go to the cinema?'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, where movies are watched.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è l'opera che vedi al cinema.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! 'There's a good movie'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the work you see at the cinema.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dove suonano le band.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Live music.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, where bands play.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, celebrazione sociale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! 'There's a party tonight'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, social celebration.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il giorno in cui sei nato.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! The occasion for many invitations.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the day you were born.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Il verbo principale di questo scenario.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedere a qualcuno di venire.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! The main verb of this scenario.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, to ask someone to come.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dare un suggerimento.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! 'I propose an idea to you'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, to give a suggestion.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Dire di sì a un invito.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, acconsentire.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To say yes to an invitation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, to consent.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, non accettare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! To say no.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, to not accept.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dare la conferma definitiva.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! 'I'll confirm to you tomorrow'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, to give definitive confirmation.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stabilire un momento o un luogo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! 'Let's set the time'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, to establish a time or place.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è sinonimo di incontrarsi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! 'We'll catch each other later'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a synonym for meeting up.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, l'atto di uscire socialmente.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! 'Shall we organize an outing?'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the act of going out socially.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Lo scopo degli inviti sociali.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, spasso e allegria.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The purpose of social invitations.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, fun and joy.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stare insieme agli altri.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! 'Thanks for the company'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, being together with others.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, più persone insieme.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! 'We're going out with the group'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, several people together.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la totalità delle persone.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! 'Are we all here?'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the totality of people.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, una persona non specificata.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! 'Is someone else coming?'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, an unspecified person.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, diverso da questo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! 'Someone else'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, different from this.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, in compagnia.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! 'Let's go together'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, in company.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! 'Vieni da solo o con qualcuno?'.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, senza compagnia.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! 'Are you coming alone or with someone?'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, without company.

feedback.correctItalian: Sì! Spesso usato come 'mi piacerebbe molto'.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, esprime desiderio o incertezza.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes! Often used as 'I would really like to'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it expresses desire or uncertainty.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Usatissimo per convincere gli amici.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'dai!' è un'esortazione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Very much used to convince friends.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'dai!' is an exhortation.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, introduce una conclusione o un momento.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! 'So see you tomorrow!'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it introduces a conclusion or a moment.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica accordo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! Simple acceptance.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates agreement.
